one_line_thesis: AES is a contracted renewables and U.S. utility platform with a $15.00 cash take-private pending; at a tight spread to the deal price, merger-arb return is below our hurdle. Watch.

deep_dive.executive_summary: AES is a contracted renewables and U.S. utility platform now subject to a $15.00 all-cash take-private by GIP and EQT, expected to close in late 2026 or early 2027. At today's price near $14.73 per share, Marvin's base merger-arbitrage path implies about 7.1% annualized return over roughly nine months, below our ~15% bar. The 12 gigawatt backlog and AES Ohio data-center load contracts support the strategic logic of the deal, but regulatory timing and spread compression limit upside. Standalone owner cash remains negative after growth capital spending, so the operating case is secondary whil...

decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

valuation.interpretation: value_per_share is withheld while any material additive component is unpriced; priced_components_per_share is not a complete security value.

scenario_contract.rule: Cases must change cited causal operating, capital, probability, timing, or financing drivers—not only a terminal multiple.

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: Predictable cash-flow security
applicability_tests: Incremental capital can be tied to identifiable projects, cohorts, or contracted capacity.
Returns on new capital can be separated from returns on the installed base.
Contract duration, volume protection, pricing, and counterparty risk are observable.
Leverage and future owner-funded capital are explicitly deducted.
failure_modes: contract renewal or regulatory reset ignored

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: A complete security value is intentionally withheld while material components are unpriced. Attribution restarts after the first proof-complete baseline.

total_return_panel.error: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.
total_return_panel.return_contract: split-adjusted close excluding dividends; event amounts normalized for later splits; cash reinvested at ex-date close; pre-tax nominal

coverage.note: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.

valuation_decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

timeline.label: Net income moved -45.8% versus the prior period (1,679.0 to 910.0).
Basic EPS moved -47.1% versus the prior period (2.4 to 1.3).
Ricardo Falú appointed President (from COO / New Energy Technologies SBU head)
Agreed to $15.00/sh cash take-private by GIP and EQT Infrastructure VI; expected close late 2026 or early 2027
FY2025 signed 4.0 GW new renewables contracts; total contracted backlog reached 12.0 GW
Senior notes offering; highlighted 6.8 GW new contracts including AES Ohio data-center load
Restated prior-period metrics after accounting policy update (non-reliance 8-K)
Re-segmented AES Andes from Energy Infrastructure into Renewables SBU reporting
Signed 6.8 GW new contracts (4.4 GW renewables PPAs, 2.1 GW AES Ohio data-center growth, 310 MW retail supply)
AES Indiana received IURC approval for new base rates and 9.9% allowed ROE
Continued coal exit program; 13.4 GW coal retirements announced cumulatively
Accelerated renewables and corporate PPA strategy; Fluence JV scale-up for energy storage

industry.structure: AES competes as a global independent power producer and U.S. regulated utility operator, selling long-term renewable power to corporates and serving retail/load customers in Indiana and Ohio. Generation is capital-intensive with project-level non-recourse debt; U.S. utilities face state commission oversight on returns.
industry.share_shift: AES has been a top-two global seller of renewable power to corporates (BloombergNEF, cited in 10-K). Data-center load growth at AES Ohio is a share-gain vector versus peers slow to contract hyperscale demand.
industry.trend: Power demand from data centers and corporate decarbonization is pulling forward renewables and grid investment; take-private by infrastructure sponsors signals private capital appetite for contracted clean-power platforms.
